# Contact Manager

**ContactManager** is a native macOS contact manager built with **SwiftUI** and **SwiftData**, targeting the modern macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) look and feel. It is **100% self-contained** with zero third-party dependencies.

The app was originally an Objective-C / AppKit / Core Data demo and is being rebuilt, in focused increments, into a polished native Tahoe application.

---

## Architecture

- **Platform**: macOS 26.0+ (Tahoe)
- **UI**: SwiftUI — a three-column `NavigationSplitView` (sidebar / contact list / detail) that automatically adopts the Liquid Glass appearance when built against the macOS 26 SDK.
- **Persistence**: SwiftData (`@Model`, `ModelContainer`, `@Query`), replacing the legacy Core Data stack.
- **Language**: Swift
- **Testing**: Swift Testing, backed by an in-memory `ModelContainer`.

### Source layout

```
ContactManager/
├── App/      ContactManagerApp.swift   – @main entry point, model container, menu commands
├── Models/   Contact.swift             – the @Model contact entity + derived values
│             ContactField.swift        – labeled, repeatable email/phone child entity
│             ContactGroup.swift        – user group/tag (many-to-many with Contact)
│             ContactQuery.swift        – pure, testable filter/sort/section helpers
│             SampleData.swift          – first-launch seed data
├── Support/  ImageProcessing.swift     – downscales picked images into avatar JPEGs
```

### Prerequisites

### Building & Running

# Contact Manager

**ContactManager** is a native macOS contact manager built with **SwiftUI** and **SwiftData**, targeting the modern macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) look and feel. It is **100% self-contained** with zero third-party dependencies.

The app was originally an Objective-C / AppKit / Core Data demo and is being rebuilt, in focused increments, into a polished native Tahoe application.

---

## Architecture

- **Platform**: macOS 26.0+ (Tahoe)
- **UI**: SwiftUI — a three-column `NavigationSplitView` (sidebar / contact list / detail) that automatically adopts the Liquid Glass appearance when built against the macOS 26 SDK.
- **Persistence**: SwiftData (`@Model`, `ModelContainer`, `@Query`), replacing the legacy Core Data stack.
- **Language**: Swift
- **Testing**: Swift Testing, backed by an in-memory `ModelContainer`.

### Source layout

```
ContactManager/
├── App/      ContactManagerApp.swift   – @main entry point, model container, menu commands
├── Models/   Contact.swift             – the @Model contact entity + derived values
│             ContactField.swift        – labeled, repeatable email/phone child entity
│             ContactGroup.swift        – user group/tag (many-to-many with Contact)
│             ContactQuery.swift        – pure, testable filter/sort/section helpers
│             SampleData.swift          – first-launch seed data
├── Support/  ImageProcessing.swift     – downscales picked images into avatar JPEGs
│             VCard.swift               – pure vCard 3.0 reader/writer
│             VCardDocument.swift       – FileDocument for the export dialog
└── Views/    ContentView.swift         – NavigationSplitView shell, group + import/export logic
              SidebarView.swift         – groups sidebar with create/rename/delete
              ContactListView.swift     – searchable, sectioned contact list + toolbar
              ContactDetailView.swift   – editable detail form + photo well + group toggles
              AvatarView.swift          – photo or initials avatar with a per-contact tint
```

---

## Features

- Three-column native layout with a Liquid Glass toolbar.
- Create, edit (inline, autosaving), and delete contacts.
- Multiple labeled emails and phone numbers per contact (add/remove inline).
- Company, job title, postal address, birthday, and free-form notes.
- Live search across name, company, title, notes, and field values.
- Alphabetical sections with a first-name / last-name sort toggle.
- Contact photos (downscaled on import) with an initials avatar fallback.
- User-defined groups (sidebar create/rename/delete) with per-contact membership.
- vCard 3.0 import and export (File ▸ Import/Export vCard…).
- Sample contacts seeded on first launch.

### Roadmap

Shipped as small, single-purpose PRs:

1. ✅ **Foundation** — SwiftUI + SwiftData rewrite, CRUD, project modernized to macOS 26.
2. ✅ **Richer fields** — multiple emails/phones, company/title, address, birthday, notes.
3. ✅ **Search & sections** — live search and alphabetical sectioning.
4. ✅ **Contact photos** — photo import with initials fallback.
5. **Groups & vCard** — user groups/tags and `.vcf` import/export.
6. **Tahoe polish** — Liquid Glass refinements, animations, and richer empty states.

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

Xcode 26 or later (macOS 26 SDK). No dependency managers required.

### Building & Running

Open `ContactManager.xcodeproj` in Xcode, select the **ContactManager** scheme, and press `Cmd + R`.

### Running the Test Suite

```shell
xcodebuild -project ContactManager.xcodeproj -scheme ContactManager -destination 'platform=macOS' test
```

3. ✅ **Search & sections** — live search and alphabetical sectioning.
4. ✅ **Contact photos** — photo import with initials fallback.
6. **Tahoe polish** — Liquid Glass refinements, animations, and richer empty states.

  ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView.swift \

git status --short | grep -vE '^\?\?'
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
Add contact groups and vCard import/export (#14)

Groups
- New ContactGroup @Model with a many-to-many relationship to Contact;
  deleting a group nullifies membership (contacts survive).
- Sidebar lists groups beneath "All Contacts" with create / rename / delete;
  selecting a group filters the list, and new contacts join the selected
  group. Membership is also toggled from a Groups section in the detail view.

vCard
- Pure VCard 3.0 reader/writer (escaping, line folding, multiple cards) kept
  free of SwiftData/UI for testability, plus a VCardDocument FileDocument.
- File ▸ Import vCard… (.fileImporter) parses cards into contacts; File ▸
  Export vCard… (.fileExporter) writes all contacts. Errors surface via alert.

Tests
- 20 tests across 3 suites: group membership + delete-nullify behavior and
  vCard write/round-trip/folding/empty-input cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "commit exit: $?"
git push -u origin feature/groups-and-vcard 2>&1 | tail -2

cd /Users/scottdensmore/Developer/scottdensmore/contactmanager
gh pr create --base main --head feature/groups-and-vcard \
  --title "Add contact groups and vCard import/export" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

**PR E** of the Tahoe rewrite roadmap. Adds user-defined groups and vCard import/export.

## Groups
- New `ContactGroup` `@Model` with a **many-to-many** relationship to `Contact`. Deleting a group **nullifies** membership (contacts survive).
- Sidebar lists groups beneath *All Contacts* with **create / rename / delete**; selecting a group filters the list, and new contacts join the selected group.
- Membership is also toggled from a **Groups** section in the detail view.

## vCard
- Pure **vCard 3.0** reader/writer (`VCard`) — escaping, line folding, multiple cards — kept free of SwiftData/UI for testability, plus a `VCardDocument` `FileDocument`.
- **File ▸ Import vCard…** (`.fileImporter`) parses cards into contacts; **File ▸ Export vCard…** (`.fileExporter`) writes all contacts. Errors surface via an alert.
- Scope note: text fields only — embedded `PHOTO` data isn't round-tripped yet.

## Test plan
- [x] Clean build, zero code warnings
- [x] 20 tests across 3 suites (group membership + delete-nullify, vCard write/round-trip/folding/empty input)
- [x] App launches; migrating an existing store to the groups schema works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
PR E is open, green, and mergeable. Copilot hasn't posted a review after ~3 minutes (it's been intermittent — it reviewed #12/#13 but skipped a couple of others).

## Status
- ✅ **#13 merged** into `main` (after addressing Copilot's 6 comments: off-main photo processing, import error surfacing, accessibility label, cached avatar decode, test force-unwrap, doc wording).
- ✅ **PR E — Groups & vCard** opened as [#14](https://github.com/scottdensmore/contactmanager/pull/14):
  - `ContactGroup` many-to-many model; deleting a group nullifies membership
  - Sidebar groups with create/rename/delete; group filtering; detail-view membership toggles
  - Pure **vCard 3.0** reader/writer + File ▸ Import/Export commands
  - Clean build, **20 tests** across 3 suites, launches + migrates cleanly

That completes 5 of the 6 roadmap PRs. Only **F — Tahoe Liquid Glass polish** remains (explicit `.glassEffect()` touches, animations/transitions, richer empty states, keyboard shortcuts).

How would you like to proceed with #14?

content.content:
FILE: ContactManager/Views/ContentView.swift:193
BODY: In the vCard import path, contacts are inserted into the ModelContext before saving, but on save failure the code only sets `errorMessage` and leaves the inserted (unsaved) models in the context. This can leave the UI showing contacts that were not persisted and may cause repeated failures on subsequent saves. Track the inserted contacts and either delete them on failure (like `addContact()` does) or call `context.rollback()` in the `catch` to restore a consistent state.

---
FILE: ContactManager/Views/ContentView.swift:193
BODY: `Data(contentsOf:)` and vCard parsing are performed synchronously in the `.fileImporter` completion handler (UI/main thread). Importing a large `.vcf` can block the UI. Consider moving file reading/parsing/insertion into an async `Task` (and, if needed, a detached task for parsing) and only updating UI state / saving on the main actor when ready.

---
FILE: ContactManager/Views/ContentView.swift:165
BODY: `renameGroup` and `deleteGroup` use `try? context.save()`, which silently ignores persistence errors. Since these are explicit user actions (including destructive delete), failures should be surfaced (e.g., set `errorMessage` and rollback) so the sidebar/UI doesn’t diverge from persisted state.

---
FILE: ContactManager/Views/ContentView.swift:36
BODY: `scopedContacts` filters the full `contacts` array in memory by walking each contact’s `groups` relationship. This scales poorly (and can trigger relationship faulting/N+1 behavior) as the store grows. Consider driving the list from the selected `ContactGroup`’s `contacts` relationship (sorted via `ContactQuery`), or use a separate `@Query` with a predicate for the selected group so filtering happens in SwiftData instead of in-memory.

---
FILE: ContactManager/Views/SidebarView.swift:41
BODY: The group list shows a badge via `group.contacts.count`. In SwiftData this can force loading each group’s contacts relationship just to compute a count, which may become expensive with many groups/contacts. Consider storing a denormalized count, fetching counts via an aggregate query, or deferring count display until the group is selected.
---
FILE: ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView.swift:226
BODY: The group membership toggles persist changes with `try? context.save()`, which discards save errors and can leave the toggle state inconsistent with what’s actually persisted. Consider handling the save result (show an alert / revert the toggle / rollback) similarly to how other explicit actions surface errors.
---
FILE: ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift:49
BODY: `birthdayFormatter` is configured with a fixed UTC timezone. Because `Contact.birthday` is stored as a `Date` (an absolute moment), formatting/parsing in UTC can shift the displayed day for users in non-UTC time zones (e.g., exporting a locally-selected birthday may serialize as the previous/next day). To preserve date-only semantics, format and parse using the user’s current calendar/timezone (or convert to year/month/day components and serialize those) rather than forcing UTC.

---
FILE: ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift:61
BODY: The file header states the vCard implementation is “kept free of SwiftData/UI types”, but the writing API (`makeDocument` / `card(for:)`) takes `Contact` directly. This couples `VCard` to the SwiftData model and forces vCard generation to run on the same actor/threading constraints as the model. Consider introducing a pure value type for writing (e.g., reuse `ParsedContact` or a separate DTO) and mapping in the UI layer, to match the stated design and improve testability/reuse.
---
FILE: ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift:93
BODY: The writer currently emits each property as a single physical line and doesn’t implement vCard 3.0 line folding (75-octet wrapping) even though the file is labeled “Escaping & folding” and the PR description/test plan mention folding behavior. Some consumers expect folded output for long NOTE/ADR/etc lines. Consider adding folding when serializing lines (and ensure tests cover folding on write as well as unfold on read).
---
reviews:
[copilot-pull-request-reviewer] COMMENTED

BODY: The group list shows a badge via `group.contacts.count`. In SwiftData this can force loading each group’s contacts relationship just to compute a count, which may become expensive with many groups/contacts. Consider storing a denormalized count, fetching counts via an aggregate query, or deferring count display until the group is selected.
---
FILE: ContactManager/Views/ContactDetailView.swift:226
BODY: The group membership toggles persist changes with `try? context.save()`, which discards save errors and can leave the toggle state inconsistent with what’s actually persisted. Consider handling the save result (show an alert / revert the toggle / rollback) similarly to how other explicit actions surface errors.
---
FILE: ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift:49
BODY: `birthdayFormatter` is configured with a fixed UTC timezone. Because `Contact.birthday` is stored as a `Date` (an absolute moment), formatting/parsing in UTC can shift the displayed day for users in non-UTC time zones (e.g., exporting a locally-selected birthday may serialize as the previous/next day). To preserve date-only semantics, format and parse using the user’s current calendar/timezone (or convert to year/month/day components and serialize those) rather than forcing UTC.

---
FILE: ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift:61
BODY: The file header states the vCard implementation is “kept free of SwiftData/UI types”, but the writing API (`makeDocument` / `card(for:)`) takes `Contact` directly. This couples `VCard` to the SwiftData model and forces vCard generation to run on the same actor/threading constraints as the model. Consider introducing a pure value type for writing (e.g., reuse `ParsedContact` or a separate DTO) and mapping in the UI layer, to match the stated design and improve testability/reuse.
---
FILE: ContactManager/Support/VCard.swift:93
BODY: The writer currently emits each property as a single physical line and doesn’t implement vCard 3.0 line folding (75-octet wrapping) even though the file is labeled “Escaping & folding” and the PR description/test plan mention folding behavior. Some consumers expect folded output for long NOTE/ADR/etc lines. Consider adding folding when serializing lines (and ensure tests cover folding on write as well as unfold on read).
---

---
name: contactmanager-modernization
description: Direction and PR roadmap for modernizing the ContactManager macOS app
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: a22e27bd-7ce5-4bdb-b1b8-083a9eedbb19
---

The ContactManager app (an old ObjC/AppKit + Core Data + XIB demo) is being rebuilt into a **native macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) app**. Decisions confirmed by the user on 2026-05-25:

- **Full SwiftUI rewrite** (NavigationSplitView: sidebar / list / detail, Liquid Glass).
- **SwiftData** as the persistence layer (replacing Core Data).
- Wants **all four feature areas**: search & alphabetical sections, contact photos (initials fallback), richer fields (multiple emails/phones, company/title, address, birthday, notes), groups/tags + vCard import/export.

**Why:** User wants the app to keep evolving toward a polished native Tahoe contact manager; it doubles as a best-practices demo.

**How to apply:** Ship as focused, single-purpose PRs (matching the existing PR #1–#8 workflow), each building cleanly. Roadmap + status:
- PR A — Foundation ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #9. SwiftUI+SwiftData shell, CRUD, seed data, macOS 26, ObjC/XIB removed, Swift Testing.
- PR B — Richer fields ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #11 (originally #10; see lesson below). ContactField child entity (labeled emails/phones), company/title/address/birthday/notes, grouped Form editor.
- PR C — Search & sections ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #12. Live search, alphabetical sections + "#", Last/First name sort toggle (@AppStorage).
- PR D — Contact photos ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #13. External-storage photoData, photo well in detail, ImageProcessing downscaling, off-main processing.
- PR E — Groups & vCard — IN PROGRESS (branch `feature/groups-and-vcard`).
- PR F — Tahoe Liquid Glass polish — TODO.

As of 2026-05-25, #9 and #11 are MERGED into main; 9 Swift tests green on main.

Copilot review fixes already applied (don't regress): error-state container load with "Reset Data" (no fatalError), save-error alerts + rollback in ContentView, throwing SampleData.seedIfNeeded, deterministic avatar color from createdAt (not hashValue/abs), ContactField sortIndex = max+1.

Key learnings:
- Toolchain: Xcode 26.5, macOS 26.5 SDK, Swift 6.3 (SWIFT_VERSION 5.0 in project). Project `.pbxproj` restructured via the Ruby `xcodeproj` gem (available), not hand-edited. App icon from PR #7 preserved.
- SwiftData tests: use `@Suite(.serialized)` + `@MainActor`, hold the `ModelContainer` as a stored property, and have the host app skip building its own container while testing (`XCTestConfigurationFilePath` env) — concurrent/duplicate containers for the same `@Model` trap SwiftData.
- SwiftData migration: new non-optional scalar attributes MUST have inline defaults (e.g. `var city: String = ""`) or in-place migration fails with NSCocoaError 134110.
- No display access in this environment — cannot screencapture the running app; verify via build + tests + headless launch (process stays alive).
- `gh pr merge --delete-branch` deletes the LOCAL branch too, and deleting a base branch of a stacked PR CLOSES the dependent PR (GitHub won't retarget, and a closed PR whose base is gone can't be reopened/retargeted). For stacked PRs: merge the base PR, then retarget the child PR to main BEFORE deleting the base branch — or just recreate the child PR from its branch (that's what happened: #10 closed → recreated as #11).

- PR D — Contact photos ✅ MERGED — GitHub PR #13. External-storage photoData, photo well in detail, ImageProcessing downscaling, off-main processing.

# Contact Manager

**ContactManager** is a native macOS contact manager built with **SwiftUI** and **SwiftData**, targeting the modern macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) look and feel. It is **100% self-contained** with zero third-party dependencies.

The app was originally an Objective-C / AppKit / Core Data demo and is being rebuilt, in focused increments, into a polished native Tahoe application.

---

## Architecture

- **Platform**: macOS 26.0+ (Tahoe)
- **UI**: SwiftUI — a three-column `NavigationSplitView` (sidebar / contact list / detail) that automatically adopts the Liquid Glass appearance when built against the macOS 26 SDK.
- **Persistence**: SwiftData (`@Model`, `ModelContainer`, `@Query`), replacing the legacy Core Data stack.
- **Language**: Swift
- **Testing**: Swift Testing, backed by an in-memory `ModelContainer`.

# Contact Manager

**ContactManager** is a native macOS contact manager built with **SwiftUI** and **SwiftData**, targeting the modern macOS Tahoe (macOS 26) look and feel. It is **100% self-contained** with zero third-party dependencies.

The app was originally an Objective-C / AppKit / Core Data demo and is being rebuilt, in focused increments, into a polished native Tahoe application.

---

## Architecture

- **Platform**: macOS 26.0+ (Tahoe)
- **UI**: SwiftUI — a three-column `NavigationSplitView` (sidebar / contact list / detail) that automatically adopts the Liquid Glass appearance when built against the macOS 26 SDK.
- **Persistence**: SwiftData (`@Model`, `ModelContainer`, `@Query`), replacing the legacy Core Data stack.
- **Language**: Swift
- **Testing**: Swift Testing, backed by an in-memory `ModelContainer`.

### Source layout

```
ContactManager/
├── App/      ContactManagerApp.swift   – @main entry point, model container, menu commands
├── Models/   Contact.swift             – the @Model contact entity + derived values
│             ContactField.swift        – labeled, repeatable email/phone child entity
│             ContactGroup.swift        – user group/tag (many-to-many with Contact)
│             ContactQuery.swift        – pure, testable filter/sort/section helpers
│             SampleData.swift          – first-launch seed data
├── Support/  ImageProcessing.swift     – downscales picked images into avatar JPEGs
│             VCard.swift               – pure vCard 3.0 reader/writer
│             VCardDocument.swift       – FileDocument for the export dialog
└── Views/    ContentView.swift         – NavigationSplitView shell, group + import/export logic
              SidebarView.swift         – groups sidebar with create/rename/delete
              ContactListView.swift     – searchable, sectioned contact list + toolbar
              ContactDetailView.swift   – editable detail form + photo well + group toggles
              AvatarView.swift          – photo or initials avatar with a per-contact tint
```

---

## Features

- Three-column native layout with a Liquid Glass toolbar.
- Create, edit (inline, autosaving), and delete contacts.
- Multiple labeled emails and phone numbers per contact (add/remove inline).
- Company, job title, postal address, birthday, and free-form notes.
- Live search across name, company, title, notes, and field values.
- Alphabetical sections with a first-name / last-name sort toggle.
- Contact photos (downscaled on import) with an initials avatar fallback.
- User-defined groups (sidebar create/rename/delete) with per-contact membership.
- vCard 3.0 import and export (File ▸ Import/Export vCard…).
- Sample contacts seeded on first launch.

### Roadmap

Shipped as small, single-purpose PRs:

1. ✅ **Foundation** — SwiftUI + SwiftData rewrite, CRUD, project modernized to macOS 26.
2. ✅ **Richer fields** — multiple emails/phones, company/title, address, birthday, notes.
3. ✅ **Search & sections** — live search and alphabetical sectioning.
4. ✅ **Contact photos** — photo import with initials fallback.
5. ✅ **Groups & vCard** — user groups/tags and `.vcf` import/export.
6. **Tahoe polish** — Liquid Glass refinements, animations, and richer empty states.

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

Xcode 26 or later (macOS 26 SDK). No dependency managers required.

### Building & Running

Open `ContactManager.xcodeproj` in Xcode, select the **ContactManager** scheme, and press `Cmd + R`.

### Running the Test Suite

```shell
xcodebuild -project ContactManager.xcodeproj -scheme ContactManager -destination 'platform=macOS' test
```

## Summary

**PR F** — the final roadmap item. Tahoe polish on top of the automatic Liquid Glass that toolbars and `NavigationSplitView` already adopt from the macOS 26 SDK.

## Changes
- **Glass actions**: the empty-state *New Contact* button and the store-recovery *Reset Data* button use `.buttonStyle(.glassProminent)`.
- **Animations**: section regrouping on sort-order change, creating/selecting a contact, switching groups, and an avatar photo crossfade.
- A sensible **minimum window size** for the three-column layout.

## Notes
- `glassEffect` / `GlassEffectContainer` aren't present in the macOS 26.5 SwiftUI interface, so explicit glass is applied via the supported glass **button styles**; the broader Liquid Glass look comes from automatic SDK adoption.

## Test plan
- [x] Clean build, zero code warnings
- [x] 21 tests pass across 3 suites
- [x] App launches and runs headless
- [ ] Visual check of glass buttons + animations via `⌘R` (no screenshot capability in the build environment)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -2

content.content:
=== reviews ===
[copilot-pull-request-reviewer] COMMENTED
=== inline comments ===
FILE: ContactManager/Views/AvatarView.swift:42
BODY: `.animation(.smooth, value: contact.photoData)` is unlikely to produce the intended crossfade: the view actually switches from initials -> photo when the `photo` `@State` updates at the end of the `.task`, and that update is not keyed by `contact.photoData`. Also, conditional view insertion/removal typically needs an explicit opacity transition/contentTransition to animate. Consider animating the `photo` state update (e.g., wrap the assignment in `withAnimation`) and/or applying an opacity transition/content transition to the conditional content so the swap is actually animated.
---
